hero.title: Brenon Araujo
hero.subtitle: Backend engineer, content creator, and home‑cloud builder. I share the journey of running a personal cloud on mini‑computers and writing about the craft along the way.

aboutMe.title: Hi, I'm Brenon
bio: I'm a backend engineer in love with distributed systems, clean architecture, and the craft of writing software that lasts.

Brenon.Cloud started as a curiosity: could I run, at home, on a handful of mini‑computers, the kind of services we usually rent from big providers? Turns out yes — and along the way I'm sharing every lesson, mistake, and idea that comes up.
Beyond writing code, I write about it. Use this space as my open notebook: studies, experiments, deep‑dives, and the path I'm walking as an engineer.

blogTeaser.title: Notes from the journey
blogTeaser.description: Deep‑dives, lessons learned, and experiments — written as I go.

docker.title: What runs on my home cloud
docker.description: These are the building blocks I use to deploy, route, secure, and observe everything running on Brenon.Cloud — and what I write about most often.

managed.title: Managed with Portainer
managed.description: Visual UI to deploy and manage Swarm stacks, networks, volumes, and secrets across nodes.

stacks.title: Swarm Stacks
stacks.description: Compose v3 with deploy policies, health checks, and rolling updates for reliable releases.

scalable.title: Scalable & Resilient
scalable.description: Scale replicas per service and leverage node failover to keep apps available.

howItWorks.title: Deploy Any Service with Docker
howItWorks.description: I run Docker Swarm on a cluster of mini‑computer nodes and manage it with Portainer. Compose stacks go in, replicas scale, and rolling updates happen from a simple UI.

about.title: The infrastructure behind it
about.description: A look under the hood of the home cloud powering this site
about.vision: Brenon.Cloud started with a simple question: how far can a few mini‑computers at home go? It evolved into a small but real platform — and into the playground where I write, experiment, and learn in the open.

pathToGlory.title: Path to Glory
pathToGlory.subtitle: The roadmaps and resources I follow on the way to becoming a better engineer.
pathToGlory.intro: This is the curated trail I'm walking — and recommend — for anyone aiming for a career like mine. Roadmaps from roadmap.sh that I keep coming back to, plus the books, courses, and channels that shaped how I think.

backend.title: Backend

data-engineer.title: Data Engineer

software-architect.title: Software Architect
software-architect.description: Stepping back to design systems: trade‑offs, patterns, and the long view of a codebase.

resources.title: Study resources

authentik.title: Identity & Access (Authentik)
authentik.description: Centralize login with SSO, MFA, and fine‑grained policies for your apps and services.

uptime-kuma.title: Monitoring (Uptime Kuma)

grafana.description: Visualize metrics and logs across services with reusable, shareable dashboards.

n8n.title: Automation (n8n)
n8n.description: Automate tasks with visual workflows that connect APIs, databases, and services.

console-air.title: Akash Console Air
console-air.description: Self-custodial deployments on the Akash Network — connect a wallet, swap AKT for ACTs, and deploy without email or credit card.
